title: Baby Changing on Board Act
plain_summary: This bill says that Amtrak must put baby changing tables in their train bathrooms. Every train car needs to have at least one bathroom with a changing table. The bathrooms must have clear signs that show people where to find the changing tables. This rule only applies to new trains that Amtrak buys after this law is passed. The goal is to make it easier for parents traveling with babies on trains.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Baby Changing on Board Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Installation of Baby Changing Tables on Amtrak Trains
sections.text: This section adds new rules to federal transportation law about baby changing tables on Amtrak trains. The new section explains what different terms mean. An ADA-compliant restroom is a bathroom that follows the rules in the Americans with Disabilities Act to be accessible for people with disabilities. A baby changing table is a raised surface that can hold a child weighing up to thirty pounds while lying down so someone can change their diaper. This includes tables that pull out or fold down from the wall. A covered passenger rail train means any train that Amtrak owns and operates, and that Amtrak ordered to buy after this law passes. It does not include trains that Amtrak runs but does not own. The new rules say that all covered trains must have a baby changing table in at least one bathroom in each train car. This includes having one in a bathroom that meets disability access rules. Every bathroom with a baby changing table must have clear signs showing that the changing table is there. The changing tables themselves must also have clear signs marking them. This section also updates the official list of sections in this part of federal law to include the new baby changing table section.
